claim: Lakebase separates storage into three layers: object store (cheapest), middle soft-state write-through cache for OLTP latency, ephemeral compute nodes
actionable: Store data in open formats on object stores via Lakebase to reduce database costs while maintaining single-digit millisecond query latency

claim: Lakebase databases launch in less than a second and can scale from zero automatically, enabling cost-efficient on-demand infrastructure for AI applications
actionable: Use serverless Lakebase databases for workloads with variable traffic to avoid paying for idle capacity

claim: Lakebase database branching enables copy-on-write clones for development, testing, and AI agent experiments without storage overhead until data changes
actionable: Create isolated database branches for each AI agent to safely experiment with different strategies without affecting production

claim: Genie uses ensemble of agents with natural language interfaces to automatically write code for data queries, adopted by 81% of Databricks customers
actionable: Implement natural language data interfaces using Genie to let non-technical users self-serve analytics without SQL knowledge

claim: Databricks Assistant understands the data in your organization and can diagnose errors, explain code, and provide data-aware recommendations
actionable: Use the Databricks Assistant for in-platform AI help that understands your actual data context, not generic programming knowledge

claim: Agent Bricks automatically creates custom LLM judges that are more reliable than generic benchmarks for evaluating domain-specific agent quality
actionable: Use Agent Bricks to auto-generate evaluation metrics for your specific use case instead of relying on general LLM competition benchmarks

claim: Agent learning from human feedback automatically adjusts vector indexes, prompts, tool descriptions, and data filtering based on natural language guidance
actionable: Provide natural language corrections on agent failures and let the system auto-optimize retrieval, prompts, and tool configuration

claim: Agent Bricks can deliver 10x lower costs than naive GPT-4 prompting while maintaining or improving quality on document understanding tasks
actionable: Use Agent Bricks cost-quality tradeoff graphs to find the optimal model/technique combination for your quality requirements

claim: Four primary Agent Bricks task templates: Information extraction, Knowledge assistant, Multi-agent supervisor, Custom LLM for content generation
actionable: Choose the appropriate Agent Bricks template for your use case to skip manual agent architecture design

claim: Databricks Apps had 2,500+ customers create 20,000+ applications in first 3 months, making it the fastest-growing public preview product at Databricks
actionable: Consider Databricks Apps for rapid productionization of data intelligence applications with built-in governance and deployment

claim: Production data intelligence apps require more than frontend code: security integration, governance, infrastructure, database state management, observability
actionable: Use integrated platforms like Databricks Apps instead of building custom backend to avoid expensive security/governance reimplementation

claim: App builder ecosystem supports multiple frameworks (Streamlit, Gradio, Dash, React, FastAPI, Flask) and now JavaScript/Node support
actionable: Build Databricks Apps in your preferred framework—Streamlit for simple Python, React for complex interactive UIs

claim: Databricks Apps provides one-click integration with Lakebase for operational data backend supporting application state and agent state management
actionable: Use Lakebase via Databricks Apps to persist application and agent state without managing database infrastructure separately

claim: 80% of Neon databases are now created by AI agents (up from 30% a year ago), indicating AI agents are creating many experimental/test databases
actionable: Design database infrastructure for high-frequency ephemeral database creation to support AI agent experimentation

claim: Traditional databases are poorly suited for AI agentic workflows because developers can't branch production DBs instantly like git branches
actionable: Use serverless databases like Lakebase with branching support for AI agent development instead of traditional OLTP databases

claim: Multi-agent supervisors orchestrate specialized agents (endpoints, Genie spaces, MCP servers) to break complex questions into subqueries
actionable: Build supervisor agents that decompose complex business questions and delegate to domain-expert agents (marketing, finance, R&D, etc.)

claim: Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables agents to interface with tools and data sources through a standardized protocol
actionable: Use MCP servers in Databricks to expose UC functions, Genie, and Vector Search as standardized tool interfaces for agent orchestration

claim: Unity Catalog supports both Delta Lake and Iceberg formats with nearly identical performance, enabling format-agnostic data governance
actionable: Use Unity Catalog as your catalog of record for open data formats to avoid vendor lock-in while maintaining performance

claim: Most enterprises still store data in proprietary formats despite vendor marketing around open data, creating lock-in
actionable: Store data in open formats (Delta, Iceberg) on your own cloud storage (S3, ADLS, GCS) instead of proprietary data warehouse formats

claim: Unified governance means securing all data assets (structured, unstructured, models, dashboards) centrally with lineage and business semantics
actionable: Implement unified governance across all data types (not just structured data) to prevent unauthorized data exfiltration through dashboards and models

claim: JPMorgan Chase embedded AI leadership at management table reporting to CEO, ensuring AI is discussed in every business meeting
actionable: Elevate AI/data leadership to executive level and mandate AI discussion in all major business reviews to accelerate adoption

claim: JPMorgan processes 200,000 employees using LLMs on internal proprietary data, focusing on enterprise-specific intelligence not public web knowledge
actionable: Deploy LLMs on your internal data with proper governance to unlock enterprise-specific insights that public models cannot provide

claim: Data quality and preparation is harder than model selection; enterprises struggle with fragmented data from multiple systems and mergers
actionable: Invest in data integration and preparation infrastructure before focusing on model innovation

claim: Vector search rebuilt from ground up to scale to billions of vectors with 7x lower cost than previous versions
actionable: Use Databricks vector search for large-scale semantic search in RAG applications with cost-effective scaling

claim: AI functions in SQL now support multimodal analysis (documents, images) and are 3x faster than competitive offerings
actionable: Use SQL AI functions for document and image analysis directly in your data warehouse without external API calls

claim: MLflow 3.0 redesigned for generative AI era with real-time tracking, observability, prompt registry, and generative AI evaluation
actionable: Use MLflow 3.0 to monitor production agents and generative AI applications with automated evaluation and real-time tracing
